methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $90M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Exowatt, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Hannan Happi and Jack Abraham, has raised $90M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 3 same-stage, same-sector private companies (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M) alongside a broader climate & energy comp universe of 8 private firms and 1 public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $90M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a $180M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the 3 identified comparables yields a $150M median, with a range of $110M to $200M. Both methods are applicable given Exowatt's clear stage and sector positioning.

The two methods triangulate to a median valuation of $165M, with a defensible range of $128M (low) to $207M (high). The peer-median floor of $110M and ceiling of $200M anchor downside and upside scenarios respectively. Key uncertainties include Exowatt's relative technology maturity and capital efficiency versus named peers, and the representativeness of a 3-company peer sample in an evolving frontier-tech sector.
